title: Redundancy of IT — Cluster Node Loss Events for Critical Databases

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.14 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.8.14 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.37: Redundancy of IT — Cluster Node Loss Events for Critical Databases.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.37' running on mssql:ag, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=database sourcetype=mssql:ag earliest=-90d
| where match(message,"(?i)evict|offline|lost.quorum")
| stats count by ag_name, replica_server_name
description: Tracks loss of quorum or node eviction in database clusters supporting redundancy requirements.
value: Tracks loss of quorum or node eviction in database clusters supporting redundancy requirements.
implementation: (1) Map AG to business service; (2) ITSI optional correlation; (3) DR failover evidence.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We track loss of quorum or node eviction in database clusters supporting redundancy requirements — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SQL Server AG logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `mssql:ag`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map AG to business service; (2) ITSI optional correlation; (3) DR failover evidence.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=database sourcetype=mssql:ag earliest=-90d
| where match(message,"(?i)evict|offline|lost.quorum")
| stats count by ag_name, replica_server_name
```

Understanding this SPL

**Redundancy of IT — Cluster Node Loss Events for Critical Databases (A.8.14)** — Tracks loss of quorum or node eviction in database clusters supporting redundancy requirements.

Documented **Data sources**: `mssql:ag`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SQL Server AG logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: database; **sourcetype**: mssql:ag. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=database, sourcetype=mssql:ag, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(message,"(?i)evict|offline|lost.quorum")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ag_name, replica_server_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table
